content:
# Genesis Chapter 41 — The EMET Bible (Literal)
**Translator:** EMET | **Source:** Leningrad Codex (B19A), Folios 23v–25r | **Source Images:** `source-texts/hebrew-OT/GEN_041_page_001.jpg`, `source-texts/hebrew-OT/GEN_041_page_002.jpg`, `source-texts/hebrew-OT/GEN_041_page_003.jpg`, `source-texts/hebrew-OT/GEN_041_page_004.jpg`
**Secondary Source:** Codex Vaticanus (Vat.gr.1209), Pages 39–42 | **View LXX online:** https://digi.vatlib.it/view/MSS_Vat.gr.1209/0043 | https://digi.vatlib.it/view/MSS_Vat.gr.1209/0044 | https://digi.vatlib.it/view/MSS_Vat.gr.1209/0045 | https://digi.vatlib.it/view/MSS_Vat.gr.1209/0046

---

*Pharaoh's Two Dreams — The Seven Fat and the Seven Lean (vv. 1–7)*

**41:1** And it came to pass [וַיְהִי] at the end of [מִקֵּץ] two years of days [שְׁנָתַיִם יָמִים] — and Pharaoh [וּפַרְעֹה] was dreaming [חֹלֵם] — and behold [וְהִנֵּה] he was standing [עֹמֵד] by the Nile [עַל-הַיְאֹר].

**41:2** And behold [וְהִנֵּה] — out of the Nile [מִן-הַיְאֹר] coming up [עֹלֹת] seven cows [שֶׁבַע פָּרוֹת] — beautiful of form [יְפוֹת מַרְאֶה] and fat of flesh [וּבְרִיאֹת בָּשָׂר] — and they grazed [וַתִּרְעֶינָה] among the reeds [בָּאָחוּ].

**41:3** And behold [וְהִנֵּה] — seven other cows [שֶׁבַע פָּרוֹת אֲחֵרוֹת] coming up after them [עֹלוֹת אַחֲרֵיהֶן] out of the Nile [מִן-הַיְאֹר] — ugly of form [רָעוֹת מַרְאֶה] and thin of flesh [וְדַקּוֹת בָּשָׂר] — and they stood [וַתַּעֲמֹדְנָה] beside the cows [אֵצֶל הַפָּרוֹת] on the bank of the Nile [עַל-שְׂפַת הַיְאֹר].

**41:4** And the cows ugly of form [וַתֹּאכַלְנָה הַפָּרוֹת רָעוֹת הַמַּרְאֶה] and thin of flesh [וְדַקֹּת הַבָּשָׂר] ate [וַתֹּאכַלְנָה] the seven cows beautiful of form [אֵת שֶׁבַע הַפָּרוֹת יְפֹת הַמַּרְאֶה] and fat [וְהַבְּרִיאֹת] — and Pharaoh awoke [וַיִּיקַץ פַּרְעֹה].

**41:5** And he slept [וַיִּישָׁן] and dreamed a second time [וַיַּחֲלֹם שֵׁנִית] — and behold [וְהִנֵּה] seven ears of grain [שֶׁבַע שִׁבֳּלִים] coming up [עֹלוֹת] on a single stalk [בְּקָנֶה אֶחָד] — fat [בְּרִיאוֹת] and good [וְטֹבוֹת].

**41:6** And behold [וְהִנֵּה] — seven ears thin [שֶׁבַע שִׁבֳּלִים דַּקּוֹת] and scorched by the east wind [וּשְׁדוּפֹת קָדִים] — springing up after them [צֹמְחוֹת אַחֲרֵיהֶן].

**41:7** And the thin ears [וַתִּבְלַעְנָה הַשִׁבֳּלִים הַדַּקּוֹת] swallowed the seven fat and full ears [אֵת שֶׁבַע הַשִׁבֳּלִים הַבְּרִיאוֹת וְהַמְּלֵאֹת] — and Pharaoh awoke [וַיִּיקַץ פַּרְעֹה] — and behold, it was a dream [וְהִנֵּה חֲלוֹם].

---

*The Magicians Fail — The Cupbearer Remembers Joseph (vv. 8–13)*

**41:8** And it came to pass in the morning [וַיְהִי בַבֹּקֶר] that his spirit was troubled [וַתִּפָּעֶם רוּחוֹ] — and he sent and called [וַיִּשְׁלַח וַיִּקְרָא] all the magicians of Egypt [אֶת-כָּל-חַרְטֻמֵּי מִצְרַיִם] and all her wise men [וְאֶת-כָּל-חֲכָמֶיהָ] — and Pharaoh recounted to them [וַיְסַפֵּר פַּרְעֹה לָהֶם] his dream [אֶת-חֲלֹמוֹ] — but there was none who could interpret them [וְאֵין-פּוֹתֵר אוֹתָם לְפַרְעֹה].

**41:9** Then the chief of the cupbearers spoke [וַיְדַבֵּר שַׂר הַמַּשְׁקִים] to Pharaoh [אֶת-פַּרְעֹה] saying [לֵאמֹר]: My offenses [אֶת-חֲטָאַי] I am recalling [אֲנִי מַזְכִּיר] today [הַיּוֹם].

**41:10** Pharaoh was furious [פַּרְעֹה קָצַף] with his servants [עַל-עֲבָדָיו] — and placed me [וַיִּתֵּן אֹתִי] in custody [בְּמִשְׁמַר] in the house of the chief of the guard [בֵּית שַׂר הַטַּבָּחִים] — me and the chief of the bakers [אֹתִי וְאֵת שַׂר הָאֹפִים].

**41:11** And we dreamed a dream [וַנַּחַלְמָה חֲלוֹם] in a single night [בְּלַיְלָה אֶחָד] — I and he [אֲנִי וָהוּא] — each according to the interpretation of his dream [אִישׁ כְּפִתְרוֹן חֲלֹמוֹ] we dreamed [חָלָמְנוּ].

**41:12** And there with us [וְשָׁם אִתָּנוּ] was a young man [נַעַר] a Hebrew [עִבְרִי] — a servant of the chief of the guard [עֶבֶד לְשַׂר הַטַּבָּחִים] — and we recounted to him [וַנְּסַפֶּר-לוֹ] and he interpreted for us [וַיִּפְתָּר-לָנוּ] our dreams [אֶת-חֲלֹמֹתֵינוּ] — each man according to his dream [אִישׁ כַּחֲלֹמוֹ] he interpreted [פָּתָר].

**41:13** And it came to pass [וַיְהִי] as he interpreted for us [כַּאֲשֶׁר פָּתַר-לָנוּ] so it was [כֵּן הָיָה] — me he restored [אֹתִי הֵשִׁיב] to my position [עַל-כַּנִּי] and him he hanged [וְאֹתוֹ תָלָה].

---

*Joseph Brought from the Pit — Before Pharaoh (vv. 14–16)*

**41:14** And Pharaoh sent [וַיִּשְׁלַח פַּרְעֹה] and called Joseph [וַיִּקְרָא אֶת-יוֹסֵף] — and they hastened him [וַיְרִיצֻהוּ] from the pit [מִן-הַבּוֹר] — and he shaved [וַיְגַלַּח] and changed his garments [וַיְחַלֵּף שִׂמְלֹתָיו] and came in [וַיָּבֹא] to Pharaoh [אֶל-פַּרְעֹה].

**41:15** And Pharaoh said [וַיֹּאמֶר פַּרְעֹה] to Joseph [אֶל-יוֹסֵף]: A dream I have dreamed [חֲלוֹם חָלַמְתִּי] and there is none who can interpret it [וּפוֹתֵר אֵין אֹתוֹ] — and I have heard concerning you [וַאֲנִי שָׁמַעְתִּי עָלֶיךָ] saying [לֵאמֹר]: you hear a dream [תִּשְׁמַע חֲלוֹם] to interpret it [לִפְתֹּר אֹתוֹ].

**41:16** And Joseph answered [וַיַּעַן יוֹסֵף] Pharaoh [אֶת-פַּרְעֹה] saying [לֵאמֹר]: It is not in me [בִּלְעָדָי] — God [אֱלֹהִים] will answer [יַעֲנֶה] Pharaoh's peace [אֶת-שְׁלוֹם פַּרְעֹה].

---

*Pharaoh Recounts Both Dreams in Full (vv. 17–24)*

**41:17** And Pharaoh spoke [וַיְדַבֵּר פַּרְעֹה] to Joseph [אֶל-יוֹסֵף]: In my dream [בַּחֲלֹמִי] — behold [הִנֵּנִי] I was standing [עֹמֵד] on the bank of the Nile [עַל-שְׂפַת הַיְאֹר].

**41:18** And behold [וְהִנֵּה] — out of the Nile [מִן-הַיְאֹר] coming up [עֹלֹת] seven cows [שֶׁבַע פָּרוֹת] fat of flesh [בְּרִיאוֹת בָּשָׂר] and beautiful of form [וִיפֹת תֹּאַר] — and they grazed [וַתִּרְעֶינָה] among the reeds [בָּאָחוּ].

**41:19** And behold [וְהִנֵּה] — seven other cows [שֶׁבַע פָּרוֹת אֲחֵרוֹת] coming up after them [עֹלוֹת אַחֲרֵיהֶן] — thin [דַּלּוֹת] and very ugly of form [וְרָעוֹת תֹּאַר מְאֹד] and lean of flesh [וְרַקּוֹת בָּשָׂר] — I have not seen their like [לֹא-רָאִיתִי כָהֵנָּה] in all the land of Egypt [בְּכָל-אֶרֶץ מִצְרַיִם] for ugliness [לָרֹעַ].

**41:20** And the lean [וַתֹּאכַלְנָה הַפָּרוֹת הָרַקּוֹת] and ugly cows [וְהָרָעֹת] ate the first seven fat cows [אֵת שֶׁבַע הַפָּרוֹת הָרִאשֹׁנוֹת הַבְּרִיאֹת].

**41:21** And they came into their bellies [וַתָּבֹאנָה אֶל-קִרְבֶּנָה] — and it was not apparent [וְלֹא נוֹדַע] that they had come into their bellies [כִּי-בָאוּ אֶל-קִרְבֶּנָה] — and their appearance [וּמַרְאֵיהֶן] was ugly [רַע] as before [כַּאֲשֶׁר בַּתְּחִלָּה] — and I awoke [וָאִיקָץ].

**41:22** And I saw [וָאֵרֶא] in my dream [בַּחֲלֹמִי] — and behold [וְהִנֵּה] — seven ears [שֶׁבַע שִׁבֳּלִים] coming up [עֹלֹת] on a single stalk [בְּקָנֶה אֶחָד] — full [מְלֵאֹת] and good [וְטֹבוֹת].

**41:23** And behold [וְהִנֵּה] — seven ears withered [שֶׁבַע שִׁבֳּלִים צְנֻמוֹת] thin [דַּקּוֹת] and scorched by the east wind [שְׁדֻפוֹת קָדִים] — springing up after them [צֹמְחוֹת אַחֲרֵיהֶן].

**41:24** And the thin ears [וַתִּבְלַעְנָה הַשִׁבֳּלִים הַדַּקֹּת] swallowed the seven good ears [אֵת שֶׁבַע הַשִׁבֳּלִים הַטֹּבוֹת] — and I told the magicians [וָאֹמַר אֶל-הַחַרְטֻמִּים] — but there was none who could declare it to me [וְאֵין מַגִּיד לִי].

---

*Joseph Interprets: Seven Years of Plenty, Seven of Famine (vv. 25–32)*

**41:25** And Joseph said [וַיֹּאמֶר יוֹסֵף] to Pharaoh [אֶל-פַּרְעֹה]: Pharaoh's dream is one [חֲלוֹם פַּרְעֹה אֶחָד הוּא] — what the God [אֵת אֲשֶׁר הָאֱלֹהִים] is about to do [עֹשֶׂה] he has declared [הִגִּיד] to Pharaoh [לְפַרְעֹה].

**41:26** The seven good cows [שֶׁבַע פָּרֹת הַטֹּבֹת] — they are seven years [שֶׁבַע שָׁנִים הֵנָּה] — and the seven good ears [וְשֶׁבַע הַשִׁבֳּלִים הַטֹּבֹת] — they are seven years [שֶׁבַע שָׁנִים הֵנָּה] — it is one dream [חֲלוֹם אֶחָד הוּא].

**41:27** And the seven lean and ugly cows [וְשֶׁבַע הַפָּרוֹת הָרַקּוֹת וְהָרָעֹת] coming up after them [הָעֹלֹת אַחֲרֵיהֶן] — they are seven years [שֶׁבַע שָׁנִים הֵנָּה] — and the seven ears empty [וְשֶׁבַע הַשִׁבֳּלִים הָרֵקוֹת] scorched by the east wind [שְׁדֻפוֹת הַקָּדִים] — they will be seven years of famine [יִהְיוּ שֶׁבַע שְׁנֵי הָרָעָב].

**41:28** It is the word [הוּא הַדָּבָר] that I spoke [אֲשֶׁר דִּבַּרְתִּי] to Pharaoh [אֶל-פַּרְעֹה] — what the God [אֲשֶׁר הָאֱלֹהִים] is about to do [עֹשֶׂה] he has shown [הֶרְאָה] Pharaoh [אֶת-פַּרְעֹה].

**41:29** Behold [הִנֵּה] — seven years are coming [שֶׁבַע שָׁנִים בָּאוֹת] — great plenty [שָׂבָע גָּדוֹל] in all the land of Egypt [בְּכָל-אֶרֶץ מִצְרָיִם].

**41:30** And there will arise after them [וְקָמוּ שֶׁבַע שְׁנֵי רָעָב אַחֲרֵיהֶן] seven years of famine [שֶׁבַע שְׁנֵי רָעָב] — and all the plenty [וְנִשְׁכַּח כָּל-הַשָּׂבָע] will be forgotten [נִשְׁכַּח] in the land of Egypt [בְּאֶרֶץ מִצְרָיִם] — and the famine [וְכִלָּה הָרָעָב] will consume [וְכִלָּה] the land [אֶת-הָאָרֶץ].

**41:31** And the plenty [וְלֹא-יִוָּדַע הַשָּׂבָע] will not be known [לֹא יִוָּדַע] in the land [בָּאָרֶץ] because of that famine afterward [מִפְּנֵי הָרָעָב הַהוּא אַחֲרֵי-כֵן] — for it will be very severe [כִּי-כָבֵד הוּא מְאֹד].

**41:32** And about the doubling of the dream [וְעַל הִשָּׁנוֹת הַחֲלוֹם] to Pharaoh [אֶל-פַּרְעֹה] twice [פַּעֲמָיִם] — because the matter is established [כִּי-נָכוֹן הַדָּבָר] by God [מֵעִם הָאֱלֹהִים] — and the God [וּמְמַהֵר הָאֱלֹהִים] is hastening [מְמַהֵר] to do it [לַעֲשֹׂתָהּ].

---

*Joseph's Counsel: Appoint an Overseer, Store Grain (vv. 33–36)*

**41:33** And now [וְעַתָּה] let Pharaoh look [יֵרֶא פַרְעֹה] for a man [אִישׁ] discerning [נָבוֹן] and wise [וְחָכָם] and appoint him [וִישִׁיתֵהוּ] over the land of Egypt [עַל-אֶרֶץ מִצְרָיִם].

**41:34** Let Pharaoh act [יַעֲשֶׂה פַרְעֹה] and appoint overseers [וְיַפְקֵד פְּקִדִים] over the land [עַל-הָאָרֶץ] — and take a fifth [וְחִמֵּשׁ אֶת-אֶרֶץ מִצְרַיִם] of the land of Egypt [אֶת-אֶרֶץ מִצְרַיִם] during the seven years of plenty [בְּשֶׁבַע שְׁנֵי הַשָּׂבָע].

**41:35** And let them gather [וְיִקְבְּצוּ] all the food [אֶת-כָּל-אֹכֶל] of these good years coming [הַשָּׁנִים הַטֹּבוֹת הַבָּאֹת הָאֵלֶּה] — and let them heap up grain [וְיִצְבְּרוּ-בָר] under the hand of Pharaoh [תַּחַת יַד-פַּרְעֹה] — food in the cities [אֹכֶל בֶּעָרִים] — and let them keep it [וְשָׁמָרוּ].

**41:36** And the food [וְהָיָה הָאֹכֶל] will serve as a store [לְפִקָּדוֹן] for the land [לָאָרֶץ] for the seven years of famine [לְשֶׁבַע שְׁנֵי הָרָעָב] which will be [אֲשֶׁר תִּהְיֶיןָ] in the land of Egypt [בְּאֶרֶץ מִצְרָיִם] — and the land will not be cut off [וְלֹא-תִכָּרֵת הָאָרֶץ] in the famine [בָּרָעָב].

---

*Pharaoh Appoints Joseph — The Spirit of God (vv. 37–40)*

**41:37** And the matter was good [וַיִּיטַב הַדָּבָר] in the eyes of Pharaoh [בְּעֵינֵי פַרְעֹה] and in the eyes of all his servants [וּבְעֵינֵי כָּל-עֲבָדָיו].

**41:38** And Pharaoh said [וַיֹּאמֶר פַּרְעֹה] to his servants [אֶל-עֲבָדָיו]: Can we find [הֲנִמְצָא] such a one as this [כָזֶה] — a man [אִישׁ] in whom is the spirit of God [אֲשֶׁר רוּחַ אֱלֹהִים בּוֹ]?

**41:39** And Pharaoh said [וַיֹּאמֶר פַּרְעֹה] to Joseph [אֶל-יוֹסֵף]: Since God has made known [אַחֲרֵי הוֹדִיעַ אֱלֹהִים] all this to you [אוֹתְךָ אֶת-כָּל-זֹאת] — there is none as discerning [אֵין-נָבוֹן וְחָכָם] and wise [וְחָכָם] as you [כָּמוֹךָ].

**41:40** You [אַתָּה] shall be over my house [תִּהְיֶה עַל-בֵּיתִי] — and upon your mouth [וְעַל-פִּיךָ] all my people shall be directed [יִשַּׁק כָּל-עַמִּי] — only with regard to the throne [רַק הַכִּסֵּא] will I be greater than you [אֶגְדַּל מִמֶּךָּ].

---

*Joseph Exalted Over Egypt — Signet Ring, Fine Linen, Gold Chain (vv. 41–44)*

**41:41** And Pharaoh said [וַיֹּאמֶר פַּרְעֹה] to Joseph [אֶל-יוֹסֵף]: See [רְאֵה] — I have set you [נָתַתִּי אֹתְךָ] over all the land of Egypt [עַל כָּל-אֶרֶץ מִצְרָיִם].

**41:42** And Pharaoh removed [וַיָּסַר פַּרְעֹה] his signet ring [אֶת-טַבַּעְתּוֹ] from his hand [מֵעַל יָדוֹ] and placed it [וַיִּתֵּן אֹתָהּ] upon Joseph's hand [עַל-יַד יוֹסֵף] — and clothed him [וַיַּלְבֵּשׁ אֹתוֹ] in garments of fine linen [בִּגְדֵי-שֵׁשׁ] — and placed [וַיָּשֶׂם] a gold chain [רְבִיד הַזָּהָב] upon his neck [עַל-צַוָּארוֹ].

**41:43** And he caused him to ride [וַיַּרְכֵּב אֹתוֹ] in the second chariot [בְּמִרְכֶּבֶת הַמִּשְׁנֶה] that was his — and they called out before him [וַיִּקְרְאוּ לְפָנָיו] Avrekh [אַבְרֵךְ] — and he set him [וְנָתוֹן אֹתוֹ] over all the land of Egypt [עַל כָּל-אֶרֶץ מִצְרָיִם].

**41:44** And Pharaoh said [וַיֹּאמֶר פַּרְעֹה] to Joseph [אֶל-יוֹסֵף]: I am Pharaoh [אֲנִי פַרְעֹה] — and without you [וּבִלְעָדֶיךָ] no man [לֹא-יָרִים אִישׁ] shall lift his hand [אֶת-יָדוֹ] or his foot [וְאֶת-רַגְלוֹ] in all the land of Egypt [בְּכָל-אֶרֶץ מִצְרָיִם].

---

*Joseph's Egyptian Name and Wife — Age at Appointment (vv. 45–46)*

**41:45** And Pharaoh called [וַיִּקְרָא פַרְעֹה] Joseph's name [שֵׁם-יוֹסֵף] Zaphenath-paneah [צָפְנַת פַּעְנֵחַ] — and he gave him [וַיִּתֶּן-לוֹ] Asenath [אֶת-אָסְנַת] daughter of Poti-phera [בַּת-פּוֹטִי פֶרַע] priest of On [כֹּהֵן אֹן] as a wife [לְאִשָּׁה] — and Joseph went out [וַיֵּצֵא יוֹסֵף] over the land of Egypt [עַל-אֶרֶץ מִצְרָיִם].

**41:46** And Joseph was [וְיוֹסֵף] thirty years old [בֶּן-שְׁלֹשִׁים שָׁנָה] when he stood [בְּעָמְדוֹ] before Pharaoh [לִפְנֵי פַרְעֹה] king of Egypt [מֶלֶךְ-מִצְרָיִם] — and Joseph went out [וַיֵּצֵא יוֹסֵף] from the presence of Pharaoh [מִלִּפְנֵי פַרְעֹה] and passed through [וַיַּעֲבֹר] all the land of Egypt [בְּכָל-אֶרֶץ מִצְרָיִם].

---

*The Seven Years of Plenty — Grain Stored Without Measure (vv. 47–49)*

**41:47** And the land produced [וַתַּעַשׂ הָאָרֶץ] in the seven years of plenty [בְּשֶׁבַע שְׁנֵי הַשָּׂבָע] by handfuls [לִקְמָצִים].

**41:48** And he gathered [וַיִּקְבֹּץ] all the food [אֶת-כָּל-אֹכֶל] of the seven years [שֶׁבַע שָׁנִים] that were [אֲשֶׁר הָיוּ] in the land of Egypt [בְּאֶרֶץ מִצְרָיִם] — and placed the food [וַיִּתֶּן-אֹכֶל] in the cities [בֶּעָרִים] — the food from the fields of the city [אֹכֶל שְׂדֵה-הָעִיר] that surrounded it [אֲשֶׁר סְבִיבֹתֶיהָ] — he placed within it [נָתַן בְּתוֹכָהּ].

**41:49** And Joseph stored [וַיִּצְבֹּר יוֹסֵף] grain [בָּר] like the sand of the sea [כְּחוֹל הַיָּם] — exceedingly much [הַרְבֵּה מְאֹד] — until he stopped counting [עַד כִּי-חָדַל לִסְפֹּר] because there was no number [כִּי-אֵין מִסְפָּר].

---

*Two Sons Born: Manasseh and Ephraim (vv. 50–52)*

**41:50** And to Joseph were born [וּלְיוֹסֵף יֻלַּד] two sons [שְׁנֵי בָנִים] before the year of famine came [בְּטֶרֶם תָּבוֹא שְׁנַת הָרָעָב] — which Asenath [אֲשֶׁר יָלְדָה-לּוֹ אָסְנַת] daughter of Poti-phera [בַּת-פּוֹטִי פֶרַע] priest of On bore to him [כֹּהֵן אֹן].

**41:51** And Joseph called [וַיִּקְרָא יוֹסֵף] the name of the firstborn [אֶת-שֵׁם הַבְּכוֹר] Manasseh [מְנַשֶּׁה] — For God [כִּי-נַשַּׁנִי אֱלֹהִים] has made me forget [נַשַּׁנִי] all my hardship [אֶת-כָּל-עֲמָלִי] and all my father's house [וְאֵת כָּל-בֵּית אָבִי].

**41:52** And the name of the second [וְאֵת שֵׁם הַשֵּׁנִי] he called [קָרָא] Ephraim [אֶפְרָיִם] — For God [כִּי-הִפְרַנִי אֱלֹהִים] has made me fruitful [הִפְרַנִי] in the land of my affliction [בְּאֶרֶץ עָנְיִי].

---

*The Seven Years of Famine Begin — All Nations Come to Egypt (vv. 53–57)*

**41:53** And the seven years of plenty [וַתִּכְלֶינָה שֶׁבַע שְׁנֵי הַשָּׂבָע] came to an end [וַתִּכְלֶינָה] that was in the land of Egypt [אֲשֶׁר הָיְתָה בְּאֶרֶץ מִצְרָיִם].

**41:54** And the seven years of famine [וַתְּחִלֶּינָה שֶׁבַע שְׁנֵי הָרָעָב] began to come [לָבוֹא] as Joseph had said [כַּאֲשֶׁר אָמַר יוֹסֵף] — and there was famine [וַיְהִי רָעָב] in all the lands [בְּכָל-הָאֲרָצוֹת] but in all the land of Egypt [וּבְכָל-אֶרֶץ מִצְרַיִם] there was bread [הָיָה לָחֶם].

**41:55** And all the land of Egypt hungered [וַתִּרְעַב כָּל-אֶרֶץ מִצְרַיִם] — and the people cried out [וַיִּצְעַק הָעָם] to Pharaoh for bread [אֶל-פַּרְעֹה לַלָּחֶם] — and Pharaoh said [וַיֹּאמֶר פַּרְעֹה] to all of Egypt [לְכָל-מִצְרַיִם]: Go to Joseph [לְכוּ אֶל-יוֹסֵף] — what he says to you [אֲשֶׁר-יֹאמַר לָכֶם] do [תַּעֲשׂוּ].

**41:56** And the famine was [וְהָרָעָב הָיָה] over all the face of the earth [עַל כָּל-פְּנֵי הָאָרֶץ] — and Joseph opened [וַיִּפְתַּח יוֹסֵף] all that was in them [אֶת-כָּל-אֲשֶׁר בָּהֶם] and sold [וַיִּשְׁבֹּר] to Egypt [לְמִצְרַיִם] — and the famine was severe [וַיֶּחֱזַק הָרָעָב] in the land of Egypt [בְּאֶרֶץ מִצְרָיִם].

**41:57** And all the earth [וְכָל-הָאָרֶץ] came to Egypt [בָּאוּ מִצְרַיְמָה] to Joseph [אֶל-יוֹסֵף] to buy grain [לִשְׁבֹּר] — for the famine was severe [כִּי-חָזַק הָרָעָב] in all the earth [בְּכָל-הָאָרֶץ].

---

## Philological Notes

### 41:1 — מִקֵּץ (miqqets)
"At the end of / at the close of" — from קֵץ (qets), "end, extremity." Used as a temporal marker: "at the end of two years of days." The phrase שְׁנָתַיִם יָמִים ("two years of days") is an emphatic construction — full, complete years. This same word opens the book of Daniel (Dan 1:5, etc.), and echoes the theme of divine timing after silence: two years have passed since the cupbearer's release and his failure to remember Joseph (40:23). LXX renders miqqets with μετὰ δὲ δύο ἔτη ἡμερῶν ("after two years of days"), preserving the Hebraism.

### 41:8 — חַרְטֻמֵּי (chartummei)
The chartummim (חַרְטֻמִּים) are an Egyptian professional class — scribal priests specializing in sacred texts and dream interpretation. The word is likely an Egyptian loanword (cf. Coptic *ḥr-tp*, "chief reader/lector-priest"), not derivable from Hebrew roots. They appear again in the Exodus plague narratives (Exod 7–9). LXX renders as ἐξηγητάς ("interpreters/exegetes") in this verse.

### 41:14 — מִן-הַבּוֹר (min-ha-bor)
"From the pit/dungeon." The word בּוֹר (bor) is the same used when Joseph's brothers threw him into the pit in 37:24 ("the pit was empty, there was no water in it") and when Joseph himself described his fate to the cupbearer: "I was stolen from the land of the Hebrews and here also I have done nothing that they should put me into the pit" (40:15). This triple use of בּוֹר creates a narrative arc: cast into the literal pit → imprisoned in the metaphorical pit → lifted out of the pit to Pharaoh's right hand.

### 41:16 — אֱלֹהִים יַעֲנֶה אֶת-שְׁלוֹם פַּרְעֹה
Literally: "God will answer the peace/welfare of Pharaoh." The word שָׁלוֹם (shalom) here means well-being, good outcome, welfare — not simply "peace" in the modern sense. The construction is remarkable: Joseph deflects all personal credit. The verb עָנָה (to answer) combined with שָׁלוֹם may carry the sense "God will give Pharaoh a favorable answer" — i.e., an answer that promotes his welfare. LXX: ἄνευ τοῦ θεοῦ οὐκ ἀποκριθήσεται τὸ σωτήριον Φαραω ("apart from God, salvation-welfare for Pharaoh will not be answered").

### 41:38 — רוּחַ אֱלֹהִים (ruach Elohim)
"The spirit of God." Pharaoh, a pagan king, here recognizes that Joseph possesses divine endowment beyond human wisdom. The phrase is the same used in Gen 1:2 for the Spirit hovering over the waters. This is one of only a handful of places in Genesis where a non-Israelite spontaneously acknowledges the God of Israel as the source of superhuman capacity. LXX: πνεῦμα θεοῦ ἐστιν ἐν αὐτῷ ("the spirit of God is in him").

### 41:40 — יִשַּׁק כָּל-עַמִּי (yishshaq kol-ammi)
Literally: "shall kiss / be directed by all my people." The verb נָשַׁק (nashaq) normally means "to kiss," but in this administrative context most interpreters read it as "be directed by, be obedient to" (cf. Ps 2:12). The sense is that all Egypt would look to Joseph's mouth — his word — as the governing authority. Some render "at your command all my people shall be ordered/arranged." LXX renders the entire phrase more freely: καὶ ἐπὶ τῷ στόματί σου ὑπακούσεται πᾶς ὁ λαός μου ("and to your mouth all my people shall listen").

### 41:43 — אַבְרֵךְ (avrekh)
One of the most contested words in Genesis. Proposals include: (1) Egyptian *ib-r-k*, "attention / bow the head"; (2) Akkadian *abarakku*, a high official title; (3) Hebrew cognate of birkh ("knee") = "bow the knee!" The LXX renders it simply as κῆρυξ ("herald") — suggesting the translators understood it as a title of acclamation rather than an imperative. The MT context (a cry preceding Joseph's chariot) strongly supports a public acclamation. Translation preserves avrekh as an untranslated term with bracketed explanation: see translation above.

### 41:45 — צָפְנַת פַּעְנֵחַ (Zaphenath-paneah)
Joseph's Egyptian throne name. Proposed Egyptian etymologies include: (1) *ḏd-p3-nṯr-iw.f-ʿnḫ*, "the god speaks and he lives"; (2) *ṯnṯ-p3-ʿnḫ*, "the sustainer of life." The meaning remains debated among Egyptologists; no fully satisfying parallel has been established. The LXX Vaticanus reads ψονθομφανήχ (Psonthomphanech) — a different Greek transliteration of what is likely the same Egyptian name tradition. Translation preserves the name untranslated.

### 41:51 — מְנַשֶּׁה (Manasseh) from נָשַׁה (nashah)
"For God has made me forget (נַשַּׁנִי) all my hardship and all my father's house." The name is a piel form of נָשָׁה meaning "to cause to forget." The etymology is profound: Joseph has not suppressed his past but testifies that God has granted him relief from its pain. Note the tension: he named his son "Forgetting" — yet the narrative will immediately show that he has not forgotten his family (Gen 42ff). LXX: ἐπιλαθέσθαι με ἐποίησεν ὁ θεός ("God has made me forget").

### 41:52 — אֶפְרָיִם (Ephraim) from פָּרָה (parah)
"For God has made me fruitful (הִפְרַנִי) in the land of my affliction (עָנְיִי)." The root פָּרָה (to be fruitful, bear fruit) connects to the Abrahamic promises of fruitfulness (פְּרוּ וּרְבוּ, 1:28, 9:1, 17:6). The phrase "land of my affliction" (עָנְיִי) is remarkable: even at the height of his glory, Joseph still names Egypt as a land of affliction — suggesting his heart remains tethered to his father's house. LXX: ηὔξησέν με ὁ θεός ("God has increased/multiplied me").

### 41:56 — The "all that was in them" (אֶת-כָּל-אֲשֶׁר בָּהֶם)
The antecedent of the plural pronominal suffix is debated: the storehouses? the granaries? The MT is slightly compressed. The sense is clearly that Joseph opened all the storehouses and sold grain to the Egyptians. The same verb שָׁבַר (shabar, to sell grain / buy grain) will dominate the Joseph-brothers narrative in chs. 42–44.

---

## LXX Comparison Table (Vaticanus, pp. 39–42)

| Verse | MT (Hebrew) | LXX (Vaticanus Greek) | Significant Divergence |
|-------|-------------|----------------------|----------------------|
| 41:1 | וַיְהִי מִקֵּץ שְׁנָתַיִם יָמִים — "at the end of two years of days" | μετὰ δὲ δύο ἔτη ἡμερῶν — "after two years of days" | None substantive; LXX preserves the Hebraism |
| 41:2 | הַיְאֹר — "the Nile" (Heb. ye'or) | τὸν ποταμόν — "the river" | LXX uses generic "river"; MT uses ye'or, an Egyptian loanword (Coptic *iaro*) for the Nile |
| 41:8 | חַרְטֻמֵּי מִצְרַיִם — "magicians of Egypt" | τοὺς ἐξηγητὰς Αἰγύπτου — "the interpreters/exegetes of Egypt" | LXX renders chartummim as "interpreters" rather than preserving the loanword |
| 41:14 | מִן-הַבּוֹר — "from the pit" | ἐκ τοῦ ὀχυρώματος — "from the stronghold/fortress" | LXX upgrades bor to "fortress" — minimizing the dungeon imagery and the thematic pit-link to ch. 37 |
| 41:16 | אֱלֹהִים יַעֲנֶה אֶת-שְׁלוֹם פַּרְעֹה | ἄνευ τοῦ θεοῦ οὐκ ἀποκριθήσεται τὸ σωτήριον Φαραω | LXX renders σωτήριον ("salvation/welfare") for MT's שָׁלוֹם; shifts syntax slightly |
| 41:38 | רוּחַ אֱלֹהִים — "spirit of God" | πνεῦμα θεοῦ — "spirit of God" | Identical in substance |
| 41:40 | יִשַּׁק כָּל-עַמִּי — "all my people shall be directed" | ὑπακούσεται πᾶς ὁ λαός μου — "shall listen/obey" | LXX smooths the difficult nashaq; both convey obedience |
| 41:43 | אַבְרֵךְ — (avrekh, acclamation) | κῆρυξ — "herald" | LXX treats avrekh as a title/noun rather than an acclamation; MT's meaning remains debated |
| 41:45 | צָפְנַת פַּעְנֵחַ — Zaphenath-paneah | ψονθομφανήχ — Psonthomphanech | Different Greek transliteration of same Egyptian name; neither wholly matches proposed Egyptian etyma |
| 41:45 | כֹּהֵן אֹן — "priest of On" | ἱερέως Ἡλιουπόλεως — "priest of Heliopolis" | LXX uses the Greek name Heliopolis (City of the Sun) for On; MT preserves Egyptian Iunu/On |
| 41:51 | נַשַּׁנִי אֱלֹהִים — "God has made me forget" | ἐπιλαθέσθαι με ἐποίησεν ὁ θεός | Identical in substance; LXX aorist infinitive construction |
| 41:52 | הִפְרַנִי אֱלֹהִים — "God has made me fruitful" | ηὔξησέν με ὁ θεός — "God has increased me" | LXX uses αὐξάνω ("to increase/multiply") rather than a specific fruitfulness verb |
| 41:56 | וַיִּפְתַּח יוֹסֵף אֶת-כָּל-אֲשֶׁר בָּהֶם — "Joseph opened all that was in them" | ἀνέῳξεν Ἰωσὴφ πάντας τοὺς θησαυρούς — "Joseph opened all the storehouses/treasuries" | LXX specifies "storehouses" (θησαυρούς) where MT uses the compressed pronoun |
| 41:57 | כָּל-הָאָרֶץ — "all the earth" | πᾶσα ἡ γῆ — "all the earth/land" | Identical |

---

## Structural Note

Genesis 41 is the narrative pivot of the Joseph cycle. The entire arc from ch. 37 (the pit) through ch. 40 (the prison) reaches its turning point here in a single day: morning brings troubled dreams; by afternoon Joseph stands before Pharaoh; by evening he rides in the second chariot as vizier of Egypt. The chapter is architecturally balanced: Pharaoh dreams twice (vv. 1–7), the magicians fail and the cupbearer remembers (vv. 8–13), Joseph interprets (vv. 25–32) and counsels (vv. 33–36), Pharaoh exalts (vv. 37–45), and the narrative plays out across fourteen years (vv. 46–57). The number seven dominates: seven fat cows, seven lean cows, seven good ears, seven thin ears, seven years of plenty, seven years of famine — fourteen in total, the double of the symbolic completeness number.

The chapter also functions theologically as a reversal of the Tower of Babel: where humanity sought to make a name for itself by ascending (Gen 11), here a foreign power descends to acknowledge that wisdom belongs to the God of Israel. Pharaoh's question — "Can we find such a one as this, a man in whom is the spirit of God?" (v. 38) — is a pagan confession that no Egyptian wisdom can match divine endowment.
